name: Scott, John
evidence: Austin donor, ZIP 78736 (SW Austin/Oak Hill), gave $9,500 to Brigid Shea and Ann Howard 2021-2023. No occupation/employer on file and 'John Scott' is an extremely common name; could not corroborate a specific individual by ZIP, occupation, or donation pattern. Not the ex-Texas SoS John Scott (a Republican, wrong profile).

name: Scott, John
evidence: Austin donor, ZIP 78702 (East Austin), gave $6,000 to Brigid Shea and Ann Howard in 2024. No occupation/employer; 'John Scott' too common to confirm a specific person and no FEC history to triangulate. Likely a different individual from the 78736 John Scott.

name: Calhoun, John
evidence: Out-of-state donor in Jackson, MS 39201, gave $5,000 to Jeff Travillion in July 2025; a prolific political donor (235 FEC contributions). 'John Calhoun' is a very common name and searches surfaced only the historical statesman and directory listings; could not confirm this specific individual's occupation or identity.

name: Kumar, Sam
evidence: Gave $4,500 to Jeff Travillion and Ann Howard in late 2021. No ZIP, no location, no occupation, and no FEC history on file; a common name with zero corroborating detail, so identification is not possible.

name: Jeff Travillion Campaign
resolved_employer: Candidate campaign committee of Jeff Travillion, Travis County Commissioner, Precinct 1 (retired 30-yr state/city government administrator)
evidence: This is the officeholder campaign committee of Jeff Travillion, elected Travis County Commissioner Precinct 1 in 2016 (re-elected 2020, 2024); the committee made candidate-to-candidate transfers to Shea, Howard, and George Morales. PO Box 1748/78767 (Austin, TX 78768) matches a county campaign committee address.

name: Kirk Watson Campaign
resolved_employer: Candidate campaign committee of Kirk Watson, Mayor of Austin (former Texas State Senator, former Austin Mayor)
evidence: Officeholder campaign committee of Kirk Watson (Austin Mayor; former TX State Senate District 14; attorney by profession). Committee made candidate-to-candidate transfers to Travillion, Shea, and Morales; Austin, TX 78768 PO-box address consistent with a local campaign committee.

name: Hayes, Warren
evidence: Donor in Georgetown, TX 78628, gave $3,530 to Travillion and Shea in summer 2022. A Warren Hayes of Georgetown (b. 1967 Snyder, TX) is a plausible match per obituary/public records, but no occupation or employer could be confirmed, so no reliable industry classification.

name: Tsao, Eric (Mr.)
evidence: Donor in the affluent Austin 78738 area (Lakeway/Bee Cave), gave $3,500 to Brigid Shea 2016-2017. Multiple people named Eric Tsao exist but all identifiable matches are outside Central Texas (LA, Tempe, Taiwan, Torrance); could not corroborate this specific individual's occupation.

name: Dove Springs Advisory Board
resolved_employer: Community/neighborhood advisory board serving the Dove Springs area of SE Austin (ZIP 78744); George Morales served as its president
evidence: The Dove Springs Advisory Board is a SE Austin neighborhood civic organization (school-supply/backpack drives, Dove Springs Rec Center improvements) that George Morales led as president before/while running for Travis County Commissioner Precinct 4; contributions here went to Morales. Civic/neighborhood body rather than a business.

name: Serranos Cocina Y Cantina
resolved_employer: Serranos Cocina y Cantina — locally owned Austin/Cedar Park Tex-Mex restaurant chain (founded 1987; Quintanilla family)
evidence: Serranos Cocina y Cantina is a well-known family-owned Austin-area Mexican restaurant group operating since 1987 with multiple locations; ZIP 78748 (South Austin) matches its footprint. Business gave $3,243 to George Morales 2024-2025.

name: Clark, Mark
evidence: Donor in West Lake Hills 78746 (affluent Austin suburb), gave $3,060 to Brigid Shea 2022-2025. 'Mark Clark' is very common in the Austin area (a critical-care physician and a registered lobbyist Mark R. Clark both appear); none could be tied to this ZIP/donation pattern with confidence.

name: Yates, Ira
resolved_employer: Ira John Yates — oil & gas mineral-interest owner in the Yates Oil Field (Pecos County, TX); descendant of oil pioneer Ira G. Yates Jr.; team member/advocate with Commission Shift (TX Railroad Commission accountability)
evidence: Ira John Yates of Paonia, CO 81428 (ZIP matches) is a descendant of rancher/oil pioneer Ira Griffith Yates Jr. and an active mineral-interest owner in the historic Yates Oil Field; he is affiliated with Commission Shift, an oil-and-gas regulatory-accountability advocacy group (orphaned wells, riverbed lease cleanup). Distinctive name + Paonia location + oil heritage corroborate; gave $3,000 to Shea and Howard 2022-2024.
